member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.Buttons.SelectionIndicatorSettings.Background
member.summary: Gets or sets the background brush for the selection indicator in the .
member.remarks: It is applicable to only when the selection mode is set to , and for border selection, the text color of the selected item is determined by the property.
member.example:
The below examples shows, how to set the property of in the . # [XAML](#tab/tabid-1)
<button:SfSegmentedControl x:Name="segmentedControl"> <button:SfSegmentedControl.SelectionIndicatorSettings> <button:SelectionIndicatorSettings Background="Orange"/> </button:SfSegmentedControl.SelectionIndicatorSettings> <button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}"> <x:String>Day</x:String> <x:String>Week</x:String> <x:String>Month</x:String> <x:String>Year</x:String> </x:Array> </button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> </button:SfSegmentedControl>
# [C#](#tab/tabid-2)
this.segmentedControl.SelectionIndicatorSettings.Background = Brush.Orange;

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.Buttons.SelectionIndicatorSettings.TextColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the text color for the selection indicator in the .
member.remarks: It is not applicable to when the is .
member.example:
The below examples shows, how to set the property of in the . # [XAML](#tab/tabid-3)
<button:SfSegmentedControl x:Name="segmentedControl"> <button:SfSegmentedControl.SelectionIndicatorSettings> <button:SelectionIndicatorSettings TextColor="Red"/> </button:SfSegmentedControl.SelectionIndicatorSettings> <button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}"> <x:String>Day</x:String> <x:String>Week</x:String> <x:String>Month</x:String> <x:String>Year</x:String> </x:Array> </button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> </button:SfSegmentedControl>
# [C#](#tab/tabid-4)
this.segmentedControl.SelectionIndicatorSettings.TextColor = Colors.Red;

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.Buttons.SelectionIndicatorSettings.Stroke
member.summary: Gets or sets the stroke color for the selection indicator in the .
member.remarks: It is applicable to only when the selection mode is set to , , or .
member.example:
The below examples shows, how to set the property of in the . # [XAML](#tab/tabid-5)
<button:SfSegmentedControl x:Name="segmentedControl"> <button:SfSegmentedControl.SelectionIndicatorSettings> <button:SelectionIndicatorSettings Stroke="Orange"/> </button:SfSegmentedControl.SelectionIndicatorSettings> <button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}"> <x:String>Day</x:String> <x:String>Week</x:String> <x:String>Month</x:String> <x:String>Year</x:String> </x:Array> </button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> </button:SfSegmentedControl>
# [C#](#tab/tabid-6)
this.segmentedControl.SelectionIndicatorSettings.Stroke = Colors.Orange;

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.Buttons.SelectionIndicatorSettings.SelectionIndicatorPlacement
member.summary: Gets or sets the selection mode for the selection indicator in the .
member.remarks: When the selection mode is set to , the selected item's appearance is determined by the property. However, when the selection mode is set to , , or , the selected color is determined by the and properties, and for border selection, the text color of the selected item is determined by the property.
member.example:
The below examples shows, how to set the property of in the . # [XAML](#tab/tabid-7)
<button:SfSegmentedControl x:Name="segmentedControl"> <button:SfSegmentedControl.SelectionIndicatorSettings> <button:SelectionIndicatorSettings SelectionIndicatorPlacement="Border"/> </button:SfSegmentedControl.SelectionIndicatorSettings> <button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}"> <x:String>Day</x:String> <x:String>Week</x:String> <x:String>Month</x:String> <x:String>Year</x:String> </x:Array> </button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> </button:SfSegmentedControl>
# [C#](#tab/tabid-8)
this.segmentedControl.SelectionIndicatorSettings.SelectionIndicatorPlacement = SelectionIndicatorPlacement.Border;

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.Buttons.SelectionIndicatorSettings.StrokeThickness
member.summary: Gets or sets the stroke thickness for the selection indicator in the .
member.remarks: It is applicable to only when the selection mode is set to , , or .
member.example:
The below examples shows, how to set the property of in the . # [XAML](#tab/tabid-9)
<button:SfSegmentedControl x:Name="segmentedControl"> <button:SfSegmentedControl.SelectionIndicatorSettings> <button:SelectionIndicatorSettings StrokeThickness="5"/> </button:SfSegmentedControl.SelectionIndicatorSettings> <button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}"> <x:String>Day</x:String> <x:String>Week</x:String> <x:String>Month</x:String> <x:String>Year</x:String> </x:Array> </button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> </button:SfSegmentedControl>
# [C#](#tab/tabid-10)
this.segmentedControl.SelectionIndicatorSettings.StrokeThickness = 5;

member.name: T:Syncfusion.Maui.Buttons.SfSegmentedControl
member.summary: The SfSegmentedControl that allows you to display a set of segments, typically used for switch among different views. Each segment in the control represents an item that the user can select. The control can be populated with a collection of objects and a collection of strings. The SfSegmentedControl provides various customization options such as segment height, width, selection indicator settings, text and icon styles, and more. It supports setting the currently selected segment using the SelectedIndex property and provides an option to enable auto-scrolling when the selected index changes. You can also customize the appearance of individual segments using the SegmentTemplate.

The SfSegmentedControl that allows you to display a set of segments, typically used for switch among different views. Each segment in the control represents an item that the user can select. The control can be populated with a collection of objects and a collection of strings. The SfSegmentedControl provides various customization options such as segment height, width, selection indicator settings, text and icon styles, and more. It supports setting the currently selected segment using the SelectedIndex property and provides an option to enable auto-scrolling when the selected index changes. You can also customize the appearance of individual segments using the SegmentTemplate.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.Buttons.SfSegmentedControl.SegmentHeight
member.summary: Gets or sets the height of the segments in the .
member.example:
The below examples shows, how to use the segment height property in the . # [XAML](#tab/tabid-8)
<button:SfSegmentedControl x:Name="segmentedControl" SegmentHeight="60"> <button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}"> <x:String>Day</x:String> <x:String>Week</x:String> <x:String>Month</x:String> <x:String>Year</x:String> </x:Array> </button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> </button:SfSegmentedControl>
# [C#](#tab/tabid-9)
this.segmentedControl.SegmentHeight = 60;

member.summary: Gets or sets the width of the segments in the .

The below examples shows, how to use the segment width property in the . # [XAML](#tab/tabid-10)
<button:SfSegmentedControl x:Name="segmentedControl" SegmentWidth="120"> <button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}"> <x:String>Day</x:String> <x:String>Week</x:String> <x:String>Month</x:String> <x:String>Year</x:String> </x:Array> </button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> </button:SfSegmentedControl>

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.Buttons.SfSegmentedControl.VisibleSegmentsCount
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of visible segments to be displayed in the .
member.remarks: When set the property, it automatically adjusts the layout of segments. This means that the and properties will not apply, and the WidthRequest value should be divided by the to determine the width of each segment.
member.example:
The below examples shows, how to use the visible segments count property in the . # [XAML](#tab/tabid-12)
<button:SfSegmentedControl x:Name="segmentedControl" WidthRequest="102" VisibleSegmentsCount="2"> <button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}"> <x:String>Day</x:String> <x:String>Week</x:String> <x:String>Month</x:String> <x:String>Year</x:String> </x:Array> </button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> </button:SfSegmentedControl>
# [C#](#tab/tabid-13)
this.segmentedControl.WidthRequest = 102; this.segmentedControl.VisibleSegmentsCount = 2;

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.Buttons.SfSegmentedControl.SelectionIndicatorSettings
member.summary: Gets or sets the settings for the segment selection indicator, which is used to highlight the selected item in the .
member.remarks: When the selection mode is set to , the selected item's appearance is determined by the property. However, when the selection mode is set to , , or , the selected color is determined by the and properties, and for border selection, the text color of the selected item is determined by the property.
member.value: The default value of is , is "new SolidColorBrush(Color.FromArgb("#6750A4"))", is , is "Color.FromArgb("#6750A4")"> is "3"/>.
member.example:
The below examples shows, how to use the property in the . # [XAML](#tab/tabid-14)
<button:SfSegmentedControl x:Name="segmentedControl" SelectionIndicatorSettings="{button:SelectionIndicatorSettings SelectionIndicatorPlacement=Border, Stroke=Orange}"> <button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}"> <x:String>Day</x:String> <x:String>Week</x:String> <x:String>Month</x:String> <x:String>Year</x:String> </x:Array> </button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> </button:SfSegmentedControl>
# [C#](#tab/tabid-15)
this.segmentedControl.SelectionIndicatorSettings = new SelectionIndicatorSettings() { SelectionIndicatorPlacement = SelectionIndicatorPlacement.Border, Stroke = Colors.Orange };

member.remarks: This property will be applicable to only when the is set to "false"/>.

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.Buttons.SfSegmentedControl.TextStyle
member.summary: Gets or sets the style of segment item text, that used to customize the text color, font, font size, font family and font attributes.

<button:SfSegmentedControl x:Name="segmentedControl" TextStyle="{button:SegmentTextStyle TextColor=Blue, FontAttributes=Italic}"> <button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}"> <x:String>Day</x:String> <x:String>Week</x:String> <x:String>Month</x:String> <x:String>Year</x:String> </x:Array> </button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> </button:SfSegmentedControl>

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.Buttons.SfSegmentedControl.CornerRadius
member.summary: Gets or sets the corner radius for the border of the .
member.remarks: This property will be applicable to only for the first and last segment items. To set CornerRadius for all segments, use .
member.value: The default value is with a value of 20.
member.example:
The below examples shows, how to use the property in the . # [XAML](#tab/tabid-30)
<button:SfSegmentedControl x:Name="segmentedControl" CornerRadius="3"> <button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}"> <x:String>Day</x:String> <x:String>Week</x:String> <x:String>Month</x:String> <x:String>Year</x:String> </x:Array> </button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> </button:SfSegmentedControl>

this.segmentedControl.CornerRadius = 3;

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.Buttons.SfSegmentedControl.SegmentCornerRadius
member.summary: Gets or sets the segment corner radius for the segment items of the .
member.value: The default value is with a value of 0.
member.example:
The below examples shows, how to use the property in the . # [XAML](#tab/tabid-32)
<button:SfSegmentedControl x:Name="segmentedControl" SegmentCornerRadius="30" StrokeThickness="0" ShowSeparator="False"> <button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}"> <x:String>Day</x:String> <x:String>Week</x:String> <x:String>Month</x:String> <x:String>Year</x:String> </x:Array> </button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> </button:SfSegmentedControl>
# [C#](#tab/tabid-33)
this.segmentedControl.SegmentCornerRadius = 30; this.segmentedControl.StrokeThickness = 0; this.segmentedControl.ShowSeparator = false;

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.Buttons.SfSegmentedControl.AutoScrollToSelectedSegment
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to enable auto-scrolling when the selected index is changed in the .
member.remarks: This property will be applicable to only during the initial loading of the .

The below examples shows, how to use the property in the . # [XAML](#tab/tabid-34)
<button:SfSegmentedControl x:Name="segmentedControl" AutoScrollToSelectedSegment="False" WidthRequest="102" SelectedIndex="2" VisibleSegmentsCount="2"> <button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}"> <x:String>Day</x:String> <x:String>Week</x:String> <x:String>Month</x:String> <x:String>Year</x:String> </x:Array> </button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> </button:SfSegmentedControl>

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.Buttons.SfSegmentedControl.SegmentTemplate
member.summary: Gets or sets the data template to use for customizing the appearance of individual segments in the .
member.remarks: The will be set as binding context.
member.example:
The below examples shows, how to use the property in the . # [XAML](#tab/tabid-36)
<button:SfSegmentedControl x:Name="segmentedControl"> <button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}"> <x:String>Day</x:String> <x:String>Week</x:String> <x:String>Month</x:String> <x:String>Year</x:String> </x:Array> </button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> <button:SfSegmentedControl.SegmentTemplate> <DataTemplate> <Grid Background="Orange"> <Label Text="{Binding Text}" TextColor="Green" Margin="6"/> </Grid> </DataTemplate> </button:SfSegmentedControl.SegmentTemplate> </button:SfSegmentedControl>

member.name: P:Syncfusion.Maui.Buttons.SfSegmentedControl.ShowSeparator
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to show separators between segments in the .
member.example:
The below examples shows, how to use the property in the . # [XAML](#tab/tabid-37)
<button:SfSegmentedControl x:Name="segmentedControl" ShowSeparator="False" SegmentCornerRadius="30" StrokeThickness="0"> <button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}"> <x:String>Day</x:String> <x:String>Week</x:String> <x:String>Month</x:String> <x:String>Year</x:String> </x:Array> </button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> </button:SfSegmentedControl>
# [C#](#tab/tabid-38)
this.segmentedControl.ShowSeparator = false; this.segmentedControl.SegmentCornerRadius = 30; this.segmentedControl.StrokeThickness = 0;

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.Buttons.SfSegmentedControl.scrollView
member.summary: The scroll view for internal scrolling when segments exceed available width.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.Buttons.SfSegmentedControl.grid
member.summary: ToDo - Ensure that the views within the segmented control stay within the bounds of the calculated width and height. The views are clipped using a Grid element instead of directly applying the clipping to the ScrollView. The Grid container utilizes a RoundRectangleGeometry for clipping, allowing full functionality of the ScrollView on all platforms, including macOS and iOS.

member.name: F:Syncfusion.Maui.Buttons.SfSegmentedControl.keyNavigationView
member.summary: Gets or sets the navigation view that currently has keyboard focus.
member.remarks: This field represents the instance that currently possesses keyboard focus. When a navigation view has keyboard focus, it implies that it is the active element that will respond to keyboard input. This field can hold a reference to the object that has gained focus through keyboard interaction. If no navigation view currently has keyboard focus, this field can be null.

member.summary: Sets the enabled state of a specific segment at the specified index.
member.param: The index of the segment to set the enabled state for.
Determines whether the segment should be enabled (true) or disabled (false).
member.example:
The below examples shows, how to use the method to the . # [XAML](#tab/tabid-3)
<button:SfSegmentedControl x:Name="segmentedControl"> <button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}"> <x:String>Day</x:String> <x:String>Week</x:String> <x:String>Month</x:String> <x:String>Year</x:String> </x:Array> </button:SfSegmentedControl.ItemsSource> </button:SfSegmentedControl>
# [C#](#tab/tabid-4)
this.segmentedControl.SetSegmentEnabled(2, false);
